title: @property, Trig Spiral, Font Breath, Elastic Follow, Liquid Tabs
description: Five live UI demos: CSS @property rainbow chase, Fibonacci spiral grid, variable font breathing, elastic sticker cursor follow, and gooey liquid tabs.

drops.title: @property Rainbow Chase
drops.description: CSS @property registers a custom hue angle for native gradient animation — spinning conics on borders, text, and progress bars.
drops.concept: CSS @property lets the browser register custom properties with a type, enabling transitions and animations that were previously impossible. Animating --hue as an angle spins a conic-gradient around any element, creating a rainbow that chases its own tail. The same technique drives bordered cards, rainbow text, gradient buttons, and animated progress bars simultaneously — all with a single @keyframes.

drops.title: CSS Trig Spiral Grid
drops.description: CSS sin() and cos() with a Fibonacci golden angle arrange cards in a natural spiral; hover springs cards to full scale.
drops.concept: CSS trigonometric functions (sin, cos) allow computing Fibonacci-spiral positions using the 137.5° golden angle as custom properties. Cards are placed with absolute positioning driven by --x/--y custom properties derived from the angle and radius, then animated in with a spin using cubic-bezier spring easing. Hover brings any card to the foreground at full scale.

interaction.mouse: Hover any card to bring it forward at full scale

drops.title: Variable Font Breathing
drops.description: font-weight animates 100→900 per character with staggered delays, combined with scaleX and skewX for kinetic text breathing.
drops.concept: Because font-weight is a registered animatable property, CSS @keyframes can smoothly cycle it across the full weight range. Adding scaleX compression at heavy weights (wide becomes narrow as weight increases — optical sizing) and skewX to simulate slant axis creates a breathing 3D effect from a flat property. Per-character stagger with --delay custom properties creates a wave.

interaction.mouse: Hover bottom row letters to trigger weight animation

drops.title: Elastic Sticker Follow
drops.description: Emoji stickers spring toward the cursor on proximity, stretching elastically, then snap back with Hooke's law physics and a SVG bezier trail.
drops.concept: Each sticker has a spring simulation: when the cursor enters its proximity radius, a force pulls the sticker toward the cursor (capped at MAX_STRETCH distance). Damped Euler integration gives elastic overshoot. An SVG quadratic bezier from origin to current position visualizes the tension. When the cursor leaves, the spring returns the sticker to its origin with the same physics.
drops.source: Spring-based magnetic UI pattern — web physics UI trend 2026

interaction.mouse: Move near stickers to attract them; the bezier trail shows tension
interaction.touch: Touch screen to attract nearby stickers

drops.title: Liquid Gooey Tabs
drops.description: Tab indicator merges into neighboring tabs like liquid using SVG feGaussianBlur + feColorMatrix goo filter. Three styles: pill, icon, underline.
drops.concept: The classic SVG 'goo' trick applies feGaussianBlur to blur all elements together, then feColorMatrix with a high contrast value (22 -10 in alpha channel) thresholds the blurred alpha so overlapping shapes merge into organic blobs. Applied to a tab bar's indicator pill, it creates a liquid membrane effect as the pill slides between tabs. A spring cubic-bezier makes it overshoot and settle.
drops.source: SVG feColorMatrix goo filter technique + spring tab transitions

interaction.mouse: Click tabs to see liquid indicator transition
